summary: Package exfiltrates host reconnaissance data to attacker-controlled Telegram bot
details: index.js combines child_process.exec, os/hostname/id collection, and an https.get call to api.telegram.org/bot<token>, which is a well-known C2/exfiltration channel used by supply-chain attackers. The code runs shell commands to collect host identity (hostname, id) and transmits the results to a Telegram bot endpoint controlled by the author. This is an unambiguous dependency-confusion / active-attack pattern: any installer requiring this package will have host reconnaissance data beaconed to a third party. The package name and high version number (99.99.11) also resemble a dependency-confusion attempt against an internal `@corpweb-ui/*` scope.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
